# Try fetching symbols from Microsoft symbol server for the three .NET libraries
# libmonosgen-2.0.so BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
# libSystem.Native.so BuildId: 7a3b5c9d1e4f6082a3c5d7e9f1b2d4a6c8e0f213
# libSystem.Globalization.Native.so BuildId: 2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e

SYMSERVER="https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols"

# Try various URL patterns
for lib in libmonosgen-2.0.so libSystem.Native.so libSystem.Globalization.Native.so; do
  case $lib in
    libmonosgen-2.0.so) bid="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be" ;;
    libSystem.Native.so) bid="7a3b5c9d1e4f6082a3c5d7e9f1b2d4a6c8e0f213" ;;
    libSystem.Globalization.Native.so) bid="2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8c0e" ;;
  esac
  echo "=== $lib (BuildId: $bid) ==="
  # Try elf-buildid-sym pattern
  url="$SYMSERVER/$lib/elf-buildid-sym-${bid}/$lib"
  echo "Trying: $url"
  curl -sI "$url" 2>&1 | head -3
  echo ""
done

The dynamic symbols are very coarse - the offsets are huge which means these are just the nearest exported symbols, not the actual functions. Let me look more carefully at the symbols. I should use all symbols (not just dynamic) and also try to find the actual function boundaries.

Actually, since the binary is stripped, the regular symbol table is gone. Only `.dynsym` remains. The large offsets suggest these are internal functions that aren't exported.

Let me try a different approach - look at the full list of dynamic symbols sorted to find better matches. Also, let me try to get the debug info file.
